response.justification: The candidate brings genuine high-throughput systems experience (microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), strong TypeScript, and — critically for this environment — current hands-on work building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq. However, Python, a stated must-have, appears nowhere on the resume; much of his deep systems work is in DLang, a niche stack; and the resume shows little evidence of staff-level technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder leadership, or operational/on-call ownership at scale. Worth a conversation if the Python requirement is flexible, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work — directly matches the AI-native engineering baseline
Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Strong TypeScript/Node.js across product and platform work, plus long-running open-source project ownership (GISCollective on Kubernetes)
concerns: No Python experience listed anywhere — the role's primary must-have language
Limited demonstrated staff-level scope: technical strategy, driving decisions at scale, and cross-functional stakeholder management are not evidenced
Deepest systems work is in DLang and dates to 2018-2020; recent roles lean toward product feature delivery rather than distributed-systems architecture
